---
title: "SO_R037: Feature Flag Configuration"
subtitle: "Standard configuration pattern for defining platform-specific feature flags in app_config.yaml"
chapter: "CH01"
category: "rule"
number: "SO_R037"
date-created: "2025-12-26"
date-modified: "2025-12-26"
author: "Claude"
type: "rule"
law: "Structure & Organization Law"
article-number:
  number: 37
  title: "Feature Flag Configuration"
related_to:
  - "SO_P017: Platform-Specific Feature Gating"
  - "SO_P010: Config-Driven Customization"
  - "SO_R005: App YAML Configuration"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    code-tools: true
    number-sections: true
---

# Article 37: Feature Flag Configuration {#overview}

**Structure & Organization Law Article 37 (Feature Flag Configuration)**

This rule defines the standard configuration format for feature flags in `app_config.yaml` and the implementation of the `is_feature_enabled_for_platform()` function.

:::{.callout-important}
## Core Rule
Feature flags are defined in the `features:` section of app_config.yaml. Each feature specifies `enabled_for:` with a list of platform_ids or "all".
:::

# Section 1: Configuration Format {#configuration-format}

## Subsection 1: YAML Structure {#yaml-structure}

**File Location:** `app_config.yaml`

**Structure:**

```yaml
# Features section in app_config.yaml
features:
  feature_name:
    enabled_for: ["platform1", "platform2"]
```

**Complete Example:**

```yaml
app_info:
  name: "MAMBA"
  version: "1.0.0"
  language: "zh_TW"

# Feature flags section
features:
  # Only eBay has review data for Poisson analysis
  poisson_comment:
    enabled_for: ["eby"]

  # RFM analysis available for all platforms
  rfm_analysis:
    enabled_for: ["all"]

  # Customer DNA available for multiple platforms
  customer_dna:
    enabled_for: ["cbz", "eby", "amz"]

  # Experimental feature - disabled for now
  beta_dashboard:
    enabled_for: []
```

## Subsection 2: Platform IDs {#platform-codes}

**Standard Platform IDs:**

| Code | Platform | Description |
|------|----------|-------------|
| cbz  | Cyberbiz | Taiwan e-commerce platform |
| eby  | eBay     | International marketplace |
| amz  | Amazon   | Amazon marketplace |
| all  | All      | Special keyword for all platforms |

**Special Values:**

- `["all"]` - Feature enabled for all platforms
- `[]` - Feature disabled for all platforms (empty list)
- Omitted feature - Defaults to enabled (see SO_P017)

# Section 2: Function Implementation {#function-implementation}

## Subsection 1: Core Function {#core-function}

**File Location:** `scripts/global_scripts/04_utils/fn_is_feature_enabled_for_platform.R`

**Implementation:**

```r
#' Check if a Feature is Enabled for a Platform
#'
#' Checks the app_config.yaml features section to determine
#' if a specific feature is enabled for the given platform.
#'
#' @param feature_name Character. The name of the feature to check.
#' @param platform Character. The platform_id (e.g., "eby", "cbz").
#'
#' @return Logical. TRUE if feature is enabled, FALSE otherwise.
#'
#' @details
#' Default behavior:
#' - Feature not in config = TRUE (enabled)
#' - enabled_for contains "all" = TRUE
#' - enabled_for is empty = FALSE
#' - enabled_for contains platform = TRUE
#' - Otherwise = FALSE
#'
#' @examples
#' is_feature_enabled_for_platform("poisson_comment", "eby")
#' # Returns TRUE if eby is in enabled_for list
#'
#' is_feature_enabled_for_platform("new_feature", "cbz")
#' # Returns TRUE if feature not configured (safe default)
#'
#' @export
is_feature_enabled_for_platform <- function(feature_name, platform) {

  # Validate inputs
  if (is.null(feature_name) || !nzchar(feature_name)) {
    return(TRUE)  # Safe default for invalid input
  }

  if (is.null(platform) || !nzchar(platform)) {
    return(TRUE)  # Safe default for no platform
  }

  # Check if app_configs exists
  if (!exists("app_configs", envir = .GlobalEnv)) {
    return(TRUE)  # Safe default if config not loaded
  }

  # Get features config
  features <- app_configs$features
  if (is.null(features)) {
    return(TRUE)  # No features section = all enabled
  }

  # Get specific feature config
  feature_config <- features[[feature_name]]
  if (is.null(feature_config)) {
    return(TRUE)  # Feature not configured = enabled
  }

  # Get enabled platforms list
  enabled_for <- feature_config$enabled_for
  if (is.null(enabled_for)) {
    return(TRUE)  # No enabled_for = enabled for all
  }

  # Empty list = disabled for all
  if (length(enabled_for) == 0) {
    return(FALSE)
  }

  # Check for "all" keyword
  if ("all" %in% enabled_for) {
    return(TRUE)
  }

  # Normalize platform_id
  platform <- tolower(as.character(platform))

  # Check if platform is in enabled list
  return(platform %in% tolower(enabled_for))
}
```

## Subsection 2: Usage Patterns {#usage-patterns}

**In Server Logic:**

```r
observeEvent(input$platform, {
  platform_id <- input$platform

  # Check feature availability
  if (!is_feature_enabled_for_platform("poisson_comment", platform_id)) {
    # Disable UI element
    disable_menu_item("poisson_comment")

    # Show message
    showNotification(
      translate("This feature is not available for the selected platform"),
      type = "warning"
    )
  }
})
```

**In Reactive Expressions:**

```r
feature_available <- reactive({
  req(input$platform)
  is_feature_enabled_for_platform("advanced_analytics", input$platform)
})

output$feature_panel <- renderUI({
  if (feature_available()) {
    advanced_analytics_ui()
  } else {
    disabled_feature_placeholder(
      translate("Advanced analytics not available for this platform")
    )
  }
})
```

# Section 3: UI Patterns for Disabled Features {#ui-patterns}

## Subsection 1: Menu Item Disabling {#menu-disabling}

**CSS for Disabled Menu Items:**

```css
/* In www/styles.css */
.disabled-menu-item {
  opacity: 0.5;
  pointer-events: none;
  cursor: not-allowed;
}

.disabled-menu-item::after {
  content: " (unavailable)";
  font-size: 0.8em;
  color: #999;
}
```

**JavaScript for Dynamic Disabling:**

```r
# In server
observe({
  platform <- input$platform

  features_to_check <- c("poisson_comment", "advanced_segmentation")

  for (feature in features_to_check) {
    enabled <- is_feature_enabled_for_platform(feature, platform)

    if (!enabled) {
      shinyjs::runjs(sprintf(
        '$("#sidebarMenu li a[data-value=\'%s\']")
          .addClass("disabled-menu-item")
          .attr("title", "%s");',
        feature,
        translate("Not available for this platform")
      ))
    } else {
      shinyjs::runjs(sprintf(
        '$("#sidebarMenu li a[data-value=\'%s\']")
          .removeClass("disabled-menu-item")
          .removeAttr("title");',
        feature
      ))
    }
  }
})
```

## Subsection 2: Placeholder Content {#placeholder-content}

**Disabled Feature Placeholder:**

```r
disabled_feature_placeholder <- function(message) {
  div(
    class = "disabled-feature-placeholder",
    style = "text-align: center; padding: 50px; color: #999;",
    icon("ban", class = "fa-3x"),
    h4(message),
    p(translate("Please select a different platform or contact support."))
  )
}
```

# Section 4: Violations and Compliance {#violations-compliance}

## Subsection 1: Violations {#violations}

**Configuration Violations:**

```yaml
# VIOLATION: Wrong key name
feature_flags:  # Should be 'features'
  my_feature: true

# VIOLATION: Boolean instead of list
features:
  my_feature: true  # Should be enabled_for: ["all"]

# VIOLATION: Inconsistent platform_ids
features:
  my_feature:
    enabled_for: ["eBay", "CyberBiz"]  # Should be lowercase: eby, cbz
```

**Code Violations:**

```r
# VIOLATION: Direct config access without function
if ("eby" %in% app_configs$features$poisson_comment$enabled_for) {
  # Should use is_feature_enabled_for_platform()
}

# VIOLATION: Hardcoded platform check
if (platform == "eby") {
  show_feature()
}
```

## Subsection 2: Compliant Examples {#compliant}

**Correct Configuration:**

```yaml
features:
  poisson_comment:
    enabled_for: ["eby"]

  customer_dna:
    enabled_for: ["all"]

  beta_feature:
    enabled_for: []  # Explicitly disabled
```

**Correct Code:**

```r
# COMPLIANT: Using the function
if (is_feature_enabled_for_platform("poisson_comment", platform)) {
  poisson_ui()
}

# COMPLIANT: Reactive pattern
feature_state <- reactive({
  is_feature_enabled_for_platform("feature_name", input$platform)
})
```

# Section 5: Testing {#testing}

## Subsection 1: Unit Tests {#unit-tests}

**Test Cases:**

```r
test_feature_gating <- function() {
  # Mock app_configs
  app_configs <<- list(
    features = list(
      feature_a = list(enabled_for = c("eby")),
      feature_b = list(enabled_for = c("all")),
      feature_c = list(enabled_for = c())
    )
  )

  # Test specific platform

  stopifnot(is_feature_enabled_for_platform("feature_a", "eby") == TRUE)
  stopifnot(is_feature_enabled_for_platform("feature_a", "cbz") == FALSE)

  # Test "all" keyword
  stopifnot(is_feature_enabled_for_platform("feature_b", "eby") == TRUE)
  stopifnot(is_feature_enabled_for_platform("feature_b", "cbz") == TRUE)

  # Test empty list (disabled)
  stopifnot(is_feature_enabled_for_platform("feature_c", "eby") == FALSE)

  # Test non-existent feature (default enabled)
  stopifnot(is_feature_enabled_for_platform("feature_x", "eby") == TRUE)

  message("All feature gating tests passed")
}
```

# Section 6: Principle Relationships {#principle-relationships}

This rule:

1. **Implements SO_P017 (Platform-Specific Feature Gating)** with specific patterns
2. **Extends SO_R005 (App YAML Configuration)** with features section
3. **Follows SO_P010 (Config-Driven Customization)** philosophy

---

*Based on: Structure & Organization Law Article 37*
*Implements: [SO_P017 Platform-Specific Feature Gating](../principles/SO_P017_platform_specific_feature_gating.qmd)*
